title: NewsNews
content:
Iranian authorities say the attack, which took place on Saturday, killed 168 people.

US-Israeli strikes have destroyed at least 11 Iranian naval vessels, images reviewed by BBC Verify show.

A fresh wave of anti-government protests at several Iranian universities that began on Saturday has spread to more campuses.

President Donald Trump said the US "is winning again" - making a series of claims about his record in a speech which lasted almost two hours.

A second US aircraft carrier appears to be heading towards the Middle East as Washington increases its pressure on Iran.

Some 42 sanctioned tankers has passed through the Channel since 11 January, a BBC Verify analysis finds.

The images from one mortuary in Tehran were shown to families who went to identify their loved ones.

Israel placed and later moved control markers in three areas, with some placed deeper inside Gaza than maps suggested.

Distressing footage reveals nearly 200 bodies with one victim identified as young as 16.

Verified footage and satellite imagery details how the US raid on President Nicholas Maduro's compound unfolded.

Latest updates from the BBC's specialists in fact-checking, verifying video and tackling disinformation.

The US president made a series of contested claims, ranging from the status of Greenland to Nato spending.

Donald Trump has said several times that he wants to acquire Greenland. 
But does the US have the right to take it?

New plaques have appeared under the portraits of former US presidents at the White House.

BBC Verify has looked back through online archives to examine what we know about President Trump and Bill Clinton's decades-long associations with Jeffrey Epstein.

US President Donald Trump claims to have ended eight wars - is that true?

BBC Verify shares tips and tricks on how to spot AI-generated videos circulating online.

A BBC Verify investigation has revealed that the identities of British public sector workers have been cloned using AI by a Russian-linked disinformation campaign.

Fake AI videos relating to the conflict have gained over 100 million views online, BBC Verify finds.

BBC Verify has debunked videos which purport to show Pakistani attacks on Indian bases and jets crashing.

BBC Verify: Earthquake video proven fake gets millions of views

The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins looks at the questions around the way Buckingham Palace has responded to the various accusations against the King's brother.

Revelations over Peter Mandelson’s links to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein have raised questions about how he became ambassador. Our Analysis Editor Ros Atkins reports.

BBC Analysis Editor Ros Atkins explores how Americans feel about their president during a nonstop start to 2026 shaped by pivotal events at home and abroad.

Executive orders, pardons, trips and Truth Social posts - the BBC’s Analysis Editor breaks down Donald Trump’s first year back in office, in numbers.

Four images seen by BBC Verify show partially clothed women with their faces and bodies unredacted.

Twenty new photos released on Friday show Epstein in the immediate aftermath of his death.

The White House posted an edited image of Nekima Levy Armstrong after she was arrested for organising a protest in a church.

BBC analysis editor Ros Atkins looks at why tensions have been running so high in the city - and how we got here.

BBC Verify breaks down how the shooting which killed a 37-year-old woman in Minneapolis unfolded.

While planning applications have risen, home building has lowered, new data suggests.

BBC Verify has examined what we know about how the Scottish budget is likely to affect different groups across the country.

The UK accuses Al-Fateh Abdullah Idris, also known as Abu Lulu, of committing acts of violence against civilians.

There's growing concern that current tax and spending policies help pensioners, but are unfair on younger generations.

The accidental release of two prisoners from Wandsworth Prison in the last week has put a spotlight on the Ministry of Justice, following the release of Hadush Kebatu in error last month.

BBC Verify examines key areas which the government has promised to make improvements

Revelations over Peter Mandelson’s links to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein have raised questions about how he became ambassador. Our Analysis Editor Ros Atkins reports.

Trump has repeatedly insisted that European countries must spend more on defence, and there are signs that has been successful.

BBC Verify has been examining some of the Manchester United owner's claims.

Former Labour Party peer tells sex offender he found "a great place to stay" with "privacy", a document suggests.
